description: Beauty product claims emphasizing deep cleansing and purification benefits, specifically targeting impurities and excess oils for clear, clean skin results.
structural_recipe: Feature a beauty/skincare product (charcoal cleanser, facial wash) and make direct claims about purification capabilities. Structure as: [Product Name] + [Purification Verb] + [What is removed (impurities/oils)] + [Result (clear/clean skin)] + [Target audience (oily skin types)]. Include before/after visual or product showcase. Add hashtags emphasizing natural/organic qualities (#طبيعي #طبيعية).
why_it_works: Saudi consumers, particularly women, prioritize skin purity and clarity as essential beauty standards. Explicit purification claims resonate with desires for visible, immediate results. Natural ingredient positioning aligns with growing preference for halal-certified and Islamic-compliant beauty products. Direct benefit statements reduce decision friction.
transplantation_caveats: Avoid unsubstantiated medical claims that violate Saudi FDA (SFDA) advertising regulations
Ensure product is certified halal and compliant with Saudi beauty product standards
Purification messaging must be aspirational, not implying existing skin is 'impure' (cultural sensitivity)
cultural_constraints: Frame benefits around confidence and wellness, not appearance anxiety
Avoid imagery suggesting skin conditions are shameful or abnormal
Compliance with Islamic modesty standards in any before/after imagery
